creator: clefevre-lists@noos.fr
text:
justin@shiningsilence.com writes:

> 
> >Description:
> 
> Checksum for downloaded RPM (needed for install) doesn't match what's in port.

humm! I suspect you didn't understand the meaning of this sentence.
there is a checksum file (distinfo) to validate the retreived files.
so, the file (rp8_linux20_libc6_i386_cs1_rpm) you've retrieved didn't
match the recorded checksum in the distinfo file.  you have to retrieve
it again, it is broken. just to be sure, the content of the disinfo file
is :

MD5 (rp8_linux20_libc6_i386_cs1_rpm) = a6503993cc1f5d38f2bd135f6fffcf8c

if yours didn'y match this one, you'll have to update your port tree
which seems to be broken.

[snip]

well, this PR may be probably closed.

Cyrille.
--
home: mailto:clefevre@poboxes.com   UNIX is user-friendly; it's just particular
work: mailto:Cyrille.Lefevre@edf.fr   about who it chooses to be friends with.
